doc.trigger: Triggered by an incoming webhook when a Fireflies call transcript is available.
doc.overview: This workflow analyzes sales call transcripts using Fireflies, extracts Pain, Impact, and Decision (PID) insights via OpenAI, and sends automated coaching emails based on these findings. Key results and scores are logged to Supabase for future tracking and reporting. The workflow is designed to automate call reviews, provide feedback to reps, and systematically store intelligence.
phases: Data Ingestion
Captures call transcript data from Fireflies and gathers relevant rep metadata.
PID Analysis
Utilizes OpenAI to extract and score Pain, Impact, and Decision insights from calls.
Reporting & Storage
Sends PID analysis and coaching emails, then syncs the results with Supabase for record-keeping.
nodes: Receives incoming trigger containing call details for analysis.
Fetches the transcript of the specified call from Fireflies.
Cleans transcript data and maps it to the corresponding sales rep.
Requests OpenAI to extract PID data from the call transcript.
Processes and structures the extracted PID data.
Uses OpenAI to derive additional call intelligence insights.
Structures output from the call intelligence step.
Combines PID and call intelligence results.
Prepares the consolidated record for reporting and storage.
Analyzes transcript for Pain score using OpenAI.
Extracts and formats Pain score output.
Analyzes transcript for Impact score using OpenAI.
Extracts and formats Impact score output.
Analyzes transcript for Decision score using OpenAI.
Extracts and formats Decision score output.
Combines Pain, Impact, and Decision scores for unified evaluation.
Sends a summary PID report to stakeholders.
Sends automated coaching advice based on call analysis.
Checks if the call is internal and bypasses PID analysis for those cases.
Queries Supabase for sales representative details.
Links call data with associated video details if applicable.
Upserts the full call analysis record into Supabase for persistent storage.
doc.notes: Review OpenAI node usage for cost and API limits; validate Supabase endpoints and permission scopes regularly.

doc.trigger: Starts when an inbound lead is submitted to the workflow’s webhook endpoint.
doc.overview: This workflow automates the qualification of inbound leads using external data enrichment, validation, and CRM integration. It verifies and scores lead contact information, enriches lead data with third-party tools, and syncs qualified leads to Supabase and Pipedrive. The process ensures leads meet specific criteria before entering the main sales pipeline.
phases: Data Collection & Restoration
Inbound lead data is captured via webhook and prepared for processing.
Contact Verification & Enrichment
Lead contact details are verified and enriched using tools like Snov, Clay, and OpenAI.
Qualification & Scoring
Leads are evaluated against multiple criteria such as email, phone, job position, and company details to determine qualification.
CRM & Database Sync
Qualified leads are sent to Pipedrive and Supabase, while rejected or duplicate leads are handled appropriately.
nodes: Restores and preprocesses the raw webhook input data.
Requests an authentication token from Snov.io API for email verification.
Initiates email verification request to Snov.io.
Pauses to allow sufficient time for external email verification.
Retrieves the result of the email verification process.
Assesses email quality and deliverability for further qualification.
Checks if the email address meets qualification criteria.
Uses OpenAI to determine if the lead's email domain matches their company.
Validates the phone number through an external verification API.
Aggregates validation results from various enrichment nodes.
Sends a request to create a new organization in Pipedrive.
Creates a new person/contact in Pipedrive CRM.
Prepares the data structure needed for new Pipedrive deals.
Creates a deal and attaches it to the qualified organization and person in Pipedrive.
Saves the new registration record to Supabase.
Updates Supabase with hygiene check results for the lead.
Saves the qualification score and details to Supabase.
Links the Pipedrive deal ID to the lead record in Supabase.
Marks leads that fail qualification as rejected in Supabase.
doc.notes: Periodic review and update of external API credentials, validation criteria, and webhook endpoints are essential for uninterrupted workflow operation.

doc.trigger: Triggered by changes in Pipedrive CRM deals.
doc.overview: This workflow synchronizes Pipedrive CRM deal data with Supabase and records the time spent at each stage of the sales pipeline. It exists to ensure all deal progress is accurately tracked for sales analytics and automated stage timing assignments in Supabase.
phases: Trigger & Filter
Detect relevant CRM changes and filter for target sales pipelines.
Stage Timing & Processing
Determine stage entry/exit, calculate time spent, and update both Pipedrive and Supabase.
Supabase Sync
Create or update corresponding deal records in Supabase reflecting current stage and durations.
nodes: Listens for updates to deals in the target Pipedrive pipeline.
Checks if the deal belongs to the 'SALES 2026' pipeline.

Checks if the deal has already entered the SAL Commit stage.

Retrieves time in MQL Contact stage.
Gets the duration spent in SQL stage.

Retrieves duration in SAL Commit stage.
Marks the deal as lost in Pipedrive if applicable.
Marks the deal as won in Pipedrive if applicable.

Checks for transition into SAL stage.

doc.notes: Ensure field mappings and stage definitions remain consistent between Pipedrive and Supabase, and monitor API quotas for both platforms to prevent sync failures.
doc._name: Sync CRM with Supabase + Add Time per Stage

doc.trigger: Triggered by an incoming webhook when a new lead is submitted from an event or course.
doc.overview: This workflow captures leads generated from events or courses and automates their entry into the Pipedrive CRM. It processes the incoming lead data, organizes it, and creates associated records for deals, people, and organizations in Pipedrive. This ensures that event/course leads are systematically tracked and managed for sales follow-up.
phases: Intake
The workflow receives and processes incoming lead data from events or courses.
CRM Entry
Lead information is standardized and used to create or update corresponding records in Pipedrive.
nodes: Accepts incoming lead data from an external source.
Processes and formats lead data for CRM compatibility.
Applies custom logic to manipulate specific lead properties.
Combines multiple data streams before CRM creation steps.
Creates a new organization entry in Pipedrive if needed.
Creates or updates a person contact in Pipedrive.
Creates a new deal in Pipedrive linked to the lead.
Performs final processing or cleanup operations on the lead data.
doc.notes: Ensure Pipedrive API credentials are current and test webhook connectivity after any schema changes.

doc.trigger: Triggered by a Calendly event when a Discovery Call is booked.
doc.overview: This workflow automates the creation or updating of Pipedrive deals when a Discovery Call is booked, synchronizing data with Supabase. It ensures persons, organizations, and deals are accurately validated, created, verified, and aligned across sales and external CRMs for streamlined sales process management.
phases: Trigger & Data Extraction
Triggered by a Calendly event and parses event details.
Person & Deal Management
Verifies existence, validates contact, and creates or updates persons, organizations, and deals in Pipedrive.
Email & Phone Validation
Checks and verifies contact details, performing email verification and domain classification.
Activity Logging & Linking
Adds notes, logs activities, and links relevant activities to deals.
Data Normalization & Enrichment
Normalizes job positions using OpenAI and maps deal data for sync.
Supabase Synchronization
Creates or updates deal records in Supabase for data alignment.
nodes: Initiates the workflow upon a booked discovery call in Calendly.

doc.trigger: Workflow starts when any error is detected via an error trigger.
doc.overview: This workflow monitors for errors in n8n executions, automatically analyzes and attempts auto-fixes for retryable errors, and notifies relevant team members by email. It ensures reliable operations by providing prompt visibility and preventing repeated issues from going unaddressed.
phases: Error Detection
Initiates when an error occurs and filters out irrelevant workflows.
Automated Remediation & Alerting
Attempts automated fixes for retryable errors and informs stakeholders via tailored email alerts.
nodes: Detects and starts the workflow on n8n execution errors.
Filters errors to process only those from specified workflows.
Implements throttling to avoid excessive processing or duplicate alerts.
Collects metadata necessary for subsequent analysis and decisions.
Calls an external service or API to analyze the nature of the detected error.
Processes the analysis result and determines the recommended action.
Checks if the error can be automatically retried.
Attempts to re-execute the failed workflow if it is retryable.
Evaluates the outcome of the retry attempt for success or failure.
Sends a notification email about the outcome of the automated retry.
Sends an alert email for non-retryable or unrecoverable errors.
doc.notes: Keep workflow credentials for HTTP and email nodes up to date; ensure rate limiting logic is effective to prevent notification spam.

doc.trigger: Runs every 23 hours using a schedule trigger.
doc.overview: This workflow automates the generation and update of documentation for all n8n workflows, committing changes directly to a GitHub repository. It detects updates, generates documentation using OpenAI, and ensures documentation stays synchronized with the latest workflow changes.
phases: Fetch & Split Workflows
Periodically requests all workflows, splits them out, and filters for active ones.
Summarize & Detect Changes
Summarizes workflow details, checks against previously cached data, and identifies new or updated workflows.
Generate & Merge Docs
Creates documentation using OpenAI, aggregates new docs, and merges them into final HTML.
Commit Updates
Builds the HTML file, updates the cache, fetches the target file SHA, and commits the new documentation to GitHub.
nodes: Triggers the workflow on a 23-hour schedule.
Fetches all available workflows from the n8n instance.
Splits the list of workflows for processing individually.
Filters out inactive workflows from the processing stream.
Retrieves detailed information for each active workflow.
Builds workflow summaries and hashes for change detection.
Groups all summaries for bulk comparison and aggregation.
Retrieves the previous cache to detect changes since last run.
Compares new and cached summaries to identify changed workflows.
Branches execution if there are detected changes.
Splits out only the workflows that have changed.
Uses OpenAI to generate natural language documentation for workflows.
Appends workflow names to the generated documentation.
Collects new documentation snippets for final assembly.
Merges all new and existing documentation together.
Constructs the final HTML documentation file.
Prepares the latest summaries for cache update.
Saves the updated workflow summaries cache.
Fetches the GitHub file SHA for the documentation file.
Commits the updated documentation HTML to the GitHub repository.
doc.notes: Keep the OpenAI API credentials secured and be mindful of rate limits from both GitHub and n8n endpoints; verify the cache file integrity regularly for consistent change detection.
doc._name: Live update on Github documentations for N8N workflows

doc.trigger: Triggered by an incoming webhook on phone call reception.
doc.overview: This workflow processes and analyzes incoming phone calls by transcribing audio, extracting insights with AI, and scoring various call components. The results are mapped and upserted into Supabase for further reporting and tracking. It exists to automate phone call evaluation, enabling richer analytics and rep performance monitoring.
phases: Input Capture & Routing
Receives incoming call data and determines the processing path based on call type.
Audio Processing & Transcription
Downloads call audio files and converts them to text using external services.
AI-Based Analysis
Uses AI nodes to extract pain points, call intelligence, rapport, and score key sections of the call.
Result Aggregation & Storage
Merges and formats analytical output, then upserts all results into Supabase tables.
nodes: Ingests call event data through a webhook trigger.
Sanitizes incoming data and associates it with the correct sales rep.
Prepares a detailed activity log to be written to the database.

Sends the audio file to a transcription service to generate call transcripts.

Performs comprehensive call intelligence analysis on the conversation.
Formats and prepares intelligence insights for storage.
Combines multiple AI analysis outputs into a single data object.
Compiles information into a unified record for database insertion.
Rates the call opening segment via AI scoring.
Extracts and formats the opening score for later use.
Scores the pain discussion portion with AI.
Formats the pain scoring output for aggregation.

Consolidates all scored results into a unified data set.
Computes a final or composite score based on all evaluation factors.

doc.notes: Monitor external API limits for transcription and AI nodes, and review Supabase schema alignment regularly when making upstream field changes.

doc.trigger: Triggered by an incoming webhook to process call analysis requests.
doc.overview: This workflow automatically analyzes recorded phone calls to extract key insights such as pain points, opening effectiveness, objections, and rapport using OpenAI. It stores both raw and analyzed data in Supabase for later reporting and review, helping sales and management track call quality and rep performance.
phases: Input & Activity Preparation
Ingests webhook data, cleans inputs, and prepares call activity records.
Audio Processing & Transcription
Downloads and transcribes audio files to create text transcripts.
AI-Powered Analysis
Extracts insights such as pain points, intelligence, and conversation scores using OpenAI.
Results Storage
Maps and upserts relevant activity and analysis records into Supabase.

doc.notes: Ensure that Supabase API keys and OpenAI credentials remain valid; periodic monitoring is recommended as AI models or Supabase schema may change.

doc.trigger: Fires when the webhook endpoint receives a request.
doc.overview: This workflow receives enriched deal data from Clay via webhook and updates the corresponding deal in Pipedrive. It ensures CRM records are continuously enhanced with the latest external data, streamlining sales operations.
phases: Receive Enrichment Data
Collects new or updated deal information from Clay through a webhook.
Update Pipedrive
Pushes the enriched information into the relevant Pipedrive deal.
nodes: Receives incoming HTTP requests containing enriched deal data from Clay.
Updates the corresponding Pipedrive deal with the new enrichment data.
doc.notes: Ensure the webhook endpoint is securely managed and the data mapping aligns with Pipedrive field requirements.

doc.trigger: Runs on a schedule trigger every 2 hours.
doc.overview: This workflow generates vector embeddings for call transcripts stored in Supabase that do not yet have embeddings. It queries for unembedded records, batches them, sends them to OpenAI text-embedding-3-small for vectorization, and updates each record with its embedding. This powers semantic search across call transcripts in the Sales Intelligence dashboard.
phases: Fetch Unembedded Records
Queries Supabase for call_transcripts rows where the embedding column is null, up to 50 records per run.
Batch & Embed
Prepares transcript text into a batch payload and sends it to OpenAI Embeddings API (text-embedding-3-small, 1536 dimensions).
Update Records
Maps each embedding back to its source record and updates the Supabase call_transcripts table with the generated vector.
nodes: Fires every 2 hours to process new unembedded transcripts.
Queries Supabase REST API for call_transcripts where embedding is null (limit 50).
Validates results and formats transcript records into a batch array for the embeddings API.
Sends transcript texts to OpenAI text-embedding-3-small and receives vector embeddings.
Pairs each generated embedding with its source record ID for the update step.
PATCHes each call_transcripts row in Supabase with its newly generated embedding vector.

doc.trigger: Triggered by a Pipedrive activity creation event.
doc.overview: This workflow automatically links calendar meeting activities in Pipedrive to the most relevant open deal in the SALES 2026 pipeline (pipeline 33). When a meeting is created without a deal association, it extracts the prospect person ID from attendees, looks up their open deals, picks the best match (preferring same-rep ownership and most recently updated), and links the activity to that deal.
phases: Filter & Extract
Filters for meeting-type activities without a deal, then extracts the prospect person ID from the attendee list (excluding the organizer/user).
Deal Lookup
Queries the Pipedrive API for all deals associated with the prospect person, then selects the best open deal in pipeline 33 based on rep ownership and recency.
Link Activity
Updates the Pipedrive activity to associate it with the matched deal via the Pipedrive API.
nodes: Listens for new activity creation events from Pipedrive.
Checks that the activity is a meeting type and has no deal already linked.
Parses attendees to find the prospect (non-organizer with a person_id).
Ensures a valid prospect person_id was found before proceeding.
Fetches all deals for the prospect person from the Pipedrive API.
Selects the best open deal in pipeline 33, preferring same-rep ownership and most recent update.
Checks that a matching deal was found.
Updates the Pipedrive activity to link it to the selected deal.
